description_long: This object consists of three cylindrical barrels, each with a ribbed body and metallic reinforcement rings at the top and bottom. The barrels are painted in distinct colors: blue, yellow, and green, each with visible weathering, scratches, and signs of use. The blue barrel features chemical hazard symbols and the text 'CH4', the yellow barrel is marked with 'N2', and the green barrel is labeled 'AR', indicating they are used for storing different gases. The barrels have open tops with no visible lids, and the interiors appear hollow. The metallic bands provide structural reinforcement, and the barrels are likely made of painted metal. These barrels are commonly found in industrial or urban environments for gas storage or waste collection.
description: A set of three weathered, ribbed metal barrels in blue, yellow, and green, each marked with chemical symbols and reinforced with metallic bands.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three barrels side by side, with visible chemical markings and hazard symbols.
description_view_1: Side view with the barrels aligned front-to-back, highlighting the ribbed texture and metallic bands.
description_view_2: Rear view of the barrels, showing the unmarked sides and the weathered paint.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the ribbed bodies and metallic reinforcement, with the blue barrel in the foreground.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: metal reinforcement rings at top and bottom
attribute_labeling_or_markings: chemical symbols, hazard signs, and text ('CH4', 'N2', 'AR')
